subtopics.title: What's in the Box
subtopics.content:
#### Board & Core Decks
- **1** double-sided game board
- **100** cards in the Dungeon Deck

#### Reserve Cards
- **15** Mercenary cards
- **15** Explore cards
- **12** Secret Tome cards
- **1** Goblin card

#### Starting Decks
- **4 decks** of 10 cards each
- **Per deck:** 6 Burgle, 2 Stumble, 1 Cautious Advance, 1 Skillful Move
- **Total starting cards:** 40

#### Tokens & Map Items
- **7** Artifact tokens (values 5 to 30)
- **2** Master Keys
- **2** Backpacks
- **3** Crowns (values 10, 9, 8)
- **3** Monkey Idols
- **24** black Dragon cubes
- **1** black Dragon marker
- **Gold tokens** in values 1 and 5

#### Player Pieces
- **Per player:** 1 pawn + 30 Clank! cubes
- **Mastery tokens:** 1 per player (2-4 used depending on player count)

#### Other Components Mentioned in Setup
- **Dragon Bag:** 1
- **Secret pools:** Major Secret tokens and Minor Secret tokens are required for setup

subtopics.title: Board Layout
subtopics.content:
#### Player-Count Audit Reference
- **Artifact count changes** — 4 players use all 7; 3 players remove 1; 2 players remove 2.
- **Rage start changes** — Dragon marker starts on different Rage spaces by player count.
- **Mastery token count changes** — Use exactly 1 per player.

1. **Artifacts first** — Place Artifact tokens on matching numbered spaces.

--- 2 Players ---
- **Artifact removal** — Randomly remove 2 Artifacts before placing.
- **Artifacts placed** — Place 5 Artifacts on matching spaces.

--- 3 Players ---
- **Artifact removal** — Randomly remove 1 Artifact before placing.
- **Artifacts placed** — Place 6 Artifacts on matching spaces.

--- 4 Players ---
- **Artifact removal** — Remove 0 Artifacts.
- **Artifacts placed** — Place all 7 Artifacts on matching spaces.

2. **Major Secrets** — Shuffle face down, place 1 on each Major Secret space, return extras unseen.
3. **Minor Secrets** — Shuffle face down, place on Minor Secret spots (2 tokens in each room with Minor Secrets).
4. **Market setup** — Place 2 Keys, 2 Backpacks, and 3 Crowns in Market.
5. **Monkey Shrine** — Place all 3 Monkey Idols in Monkey Shrine room.

--- 2 Players ---
- **Mastery tokens** — Place 2 Mastery tokens near top-left board area.
- **Rage start** — Place Dragon marker on third Rage space.

--- 3 Players ---
- **Mastery tokens** — Place 3 Mastery tokens near top-left board area.
- **Rage start** — Place Dragon marker on second Rage space.

--- 4 Players ---
- **Mastery tokens** — Place 4 Mastery tokens near top-left board area.
- **Rage start** — Place Dragon marker on first Rage space.

6. **Bank setup** — Put Gold tokens (1 and 5) beside board.
7. **Dragon Bag** — Put all 24 black Dragon cubes in the bag.
8. **Reserve setup** — Place Goblin card and reserve piles (Mercenary, Explore, Secret Tome) near board.
9. **Dungeon Row** — Shuffle Dungeon Deck and reveal 6 cards.
10. **No starting attack** — Replace any revealed Dragon Attack cards until row has none, then shuffle replacements back into deck.
11. **Discard space** — Leave room for Dungeon discard pile next to Dungeon Deck.

subtopics.title: Player Setup
subtopics.content:
#### Each Player Takes
- **1 pawn** in their color
- **30 Clank! cubes** as personal supply
- **1 starting deck** of 10 cards

#### Starting Deck Contents
- **6** Burgle
- **2** Stumble
- **1** Cautious Advance
- **1** Skillful Move

#### Before First Turn
- **Pawn location** — Place pawn just outside dungeon at top entrance.
- **Deck prep** — Shuffle your 10-card starting deck.
- **Opening hand** — Draw 5 cards.

subtopics.title: Starting the Game
subtopics.content:
- **First player** — Choose the sneakiest player, or pick randomly.
- **Turn order** — Play proceeds clockwise.

#### Player-Count Audit Reference
- **Initial Clank differs by seat** — Seat order sets starting Clank values 3/2/1/0.

--- 2 Players ---
- **Seat 1 Clank** — Place 3 Clank! in Clank! Area.
- **Seat 2 Clank** — Place 2 Clank! in Clank! Area.

--- 3 Players ---
- **Seat 1 Clank** — Place 3 Clank! in Clank! Area.
- **Seat 2 Clank** — Place 2 Clank! in Clank! Area.
- **Seat 3 Clank** — Place 1 Clank! in Clank! Area.

--- 4 Players ---
- **Seat 1 Clank** — Place 3 Clank! in Clank! Area.
- **Seat 2 Clank** — Place 2 Clank! in Clank! Area.
- **Seat 3 Clank** — Place 1 Clank! in Clank! Area.
- **Seat 4 Clank** — Place 0 Clank! in Clank! Area.

- **Start signal** — After Clank placement, first player takes first turn.

subtopics.title: Turn Structure
subtopics.content:
1. **Play cards** — Play any/all cards from hand in any order and resolve text immediately.
2. **Spend resources** — Use Skill, Swords, and Boots on legal actions in any sequence.
3. **End turn discard** — Put all played cards into your personal discard pile.
4. **Draw new hand** — Draw 5 cards; if deck is empty, shuffle discard to form new deck.
5. **Refill row** — Refill Dungeon Row to 6 cards.
6. **Attack check** — If newly revealed cards include Dragon Attack symbols, resolve exactly 1 Dragon Attack.

#### End-of-Turn Resource Rule
- **No carryover** — Unused Skill, Swords, and Boots are lost at end of turn.

subtopics.title: Actions & Options
subtopics.content:
#### General Action Rule
- **Repeat allowed** — You may take actions multiple times in a turn if you can pay costs.

#### Acquire Deck Cards (Skill)
- **Eligible cards** — Buy blue-banner cards from Dungeon Row or yellow-banner cards from Reserve.
- **Cost source** — Pay Skill equal to card cost (lower-right corner).
- **Destination** — Place acquired card in your personal discard pile.

#### Use Device Cards (Skill)
- **Device type** — Purple-banner cards in Dungeon Row are Devices.
- **Resolution** — Pay Skill, resolve Use text immediately.
- **Destination** — Put Device in Dungeon discard pile, not your deck.

#### Fight Monsters (Swords)
- **Monster type** — Red-banner cards in Dungeon Row are Monsters.
- **Cost source** — Spend shown Swords to defeat.
- **Reward** — Gain Defeat text reward.
- **Destination** — Put defeated monster in Dungeon discard pile.
- **Goblin exception** — You may fight Reserve Goblin multiple times per turn; Goblin stays in Reserve.

#### Move Through Tunnels (Boots)
- **Normal tunnel** — Costs 1 Boot.
- **Footprint tunnel** — Costs 2 Boots.
- **Monster tunnel** — Deals shown damage unless reduced 1-for-1 with Swords.
- **Locked tunnel** — Requires owning a Master Key token.
- **Arrow tunnel** — One-way in arrow direction unless effect says otherwise.

#### Take Room Tokens
- **Entry trigger** — When you move into a room, you may take 1 token there.
- **Eligible tokens** — Minor Secret, Major Secret, Monkey Idol, or Artifact.
- **Secret reveal** — Reveal Minor/Major Secret immediately and resolve.
- **Artifact limit** — You cannot take an Artifact if you already carry one.
- **Rage increase** — Taking an Artifact moves Dragon marker up 1 Rage space.
- **Optional pickup** — You may decline to take a token.

#### Buy from Market (Gold)
- **Location requirement** — You must be in a Market room.
- **Price** — Each Market item costs 7 Gold.
- **Ownership** — Place purchased items in front of you; keep them.

#### Clank! Management
- **Positive Clank** — Add cubes from personal supply to Clank! Area.
- **Negative Clank** — Remove your cubes from Clank! Area.
- **Shortfall handling** — Extra negative Clank can cancel later Clank gained that turn.
- **Turn-end reset** — Unused negative Clank is lost at end of turn.

#### Crystal Cave Rule
- **Boot lock** — After entering a Crystal Cave, you may not use more Boots that turn.
- **Teleport exception** — Teleport effects can still move you if available.

subtopics.title: Special Mechanics
subtopics.content:
#### Dragon Attack Procedure
- **Trigger** — Dragon Attack symbol appears during Dungeon Row refill.
- **Single attack rule** — Resolve only 1 attack even if multiple symbols appear.
- **Bag load** — Move all cubes from Clank! Area into Dragon Bag.
- **Draw count** — Draw cubes equal to value at Dragon marker’s current Rage space.
- **Black cubes** — Set aside; no player damage.
- **Colored cubes** — Deal 1 damage to matching player color per cube.

#### Rage Escalation
- **Artifact trigger** — Dragon Rage increases by 1 when an Artifact is taken.
- **Egg trigger** — Dragon Rage increases by 1 when a Dragon Egg secret is found.
- **Danger curve** — Higher Rage means larger attack draws later.

#### Health & Knockout
- **Damage tracking** — Put damage cubes on your color’s Health Meter.
- **Dragon damage source** — Use colored cubes drawn from Dragon Bag.
- **Tunnel damage source** — Use cubes from personal supply if you do not spend Swords to prevent tunnel damage.
- **Knockout condition** — You are knocked out if your Health Meter is full.

#### Knockout Rescue Outcomes
- **Rescued state** — If you have an Artifact and are out of the Depths area, you are rescued and still score.
- **Unrescued state** — If you lack an Artifact, or are still in Depths, you are not rescued.

#### Deck-Building Flow
- **New card timing** — Acquired deck cards enter discard first.
- **Future use** — You can use those cards only after reshuffle and redraw.

subtopics.title: Endgame & Scoring
subtopics.content:
#### Endgame State
- **Escape status** — Players who return to the starting area outside the dungeon are safe.
- **Knockout status** — Players in unrecoverable knockout states do not compete normally in final scoring.
- **Pressure source** — Endgame intensity rises as Dragon Rage and attack frequency increase.

#### Final Scoring Sources
- **Artifact value** — Score your Artifact token value (5-30).
- **Gold** — Score 1 point per Gold.
- **Crowns** — Score printed values (10, 9, 8).
- **Monkey Idols** — Score printed token values.
- **Secret tokens** — Score any VP from Secrets plus their resolved benefits gained during play.
- **Card VP** — Score VP printed on cards you own (deck/discard/played areas), including Secret Tomes and other VP cards.
- **Persistent effects** — Score any ongoing point-granting effects from cards/items.

#### Winner & Tiebreakers
- **Primary winner** — Highest total points wins.
- **Tiebreak 1** — If tied, more remaining health wins.
- **Tiebreak 2** — If still tied, more Gold wins.
- **Final tie** — If still tied, share victory.

subtopics.title: Opening Priorities
subtopics.content:
#### First 3 Turns Focus
- **Mobility first** — Prioritize early cards that add Boots and flexible utility over pure VP cards.
- **Route planning** — Enter the dungeon only with a realistic path to return.
- **Threat control** — Keep early Clank moderate so your cubes do not flood the bag before you gain loot.

#### Early Board Value
- **Token efficiency** — Target accessible Minor/Major Secrets for tempo and survivability.
- **Depth timing** — Delay very deep pushes until your deck consistently produces movement and defense.

#### Dragon Tempo Reads
- **Row monitoring** — Track how often Dragon Attack symbols appear during refills.
- **Safety pivot** — Shift to safer lines immediately if attack cadence increases.

subtopics.title: Common Mistakes
subtopics.content:
- **Greedy deep dive** — Chasing 25/30 Artifacts before your deck supports escape often ends in Depths knockout.
- **Key neglect** — Skipping Market Key when your route needs lock tunnels wastes full turns.
- **Deck bloat** — Buying too many weak cards dilutes critical movement and control draws.
- **Sword underuse** — Treating Swords as optional in dangerous corridors bleeds health too quickly.
- **Late escape pivot** — Staying underground one turn too long often loses more points than extra loot gains.

subtopics.title: Key Decisions
subtopics.content:
#### Decision 1: Low Artifact Route vs Deep Artifact Route
- **Low route** — Lower base VP, stronger survival odds, faster escape consistency.
- **Deep route** — Higher VP ceiling, much higher knockout risk, stricter deck requirements.

#### Decision 2: Quiet Deck vs Explosive Deck
- **Quiet deck** — Lower dragon exposure and steadier survival curve.
- **Explosive deck** — Higher turn spikes but higher Clank volatility.

#### Decision 3: Keep Delving vs Escape Now
- **Keep delving** — Choose only when health, deck cycle, and board position support a safe return.
- **Escape now** — Choose when Rage is rising and one bad attack could end your scoring run.

metadata.notes: Primary content written from provided Clank! rulebook excerpt and setup/action text.
